results.possibleIdentity:
Robert Safir was an electrical engineer and technical professional active in New York during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He is identified as an engineer associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. According to historical records from the E.A.T. technical services directory, Safir was registered as an Electrical Engineer specializing in electrical engineering, based in New York, NY. 

Safir's professional technical career also included a significant role at Genigraphics (originally a business unit of General Electric), where he was known as "Wizard #14." In this capacity, he managed the design and installation for the Genigraphics Business Unit (GBU), a pioneer in computer graphics systems used for high-end presentation slides and multimedia. Later in his career, he transitioned into the music industry as a veteran audio engineer, producer, and composer. He authored several technical and business guides for musicians, including "Make Your Music Video and Put It Online" (2010) and "Marketing and Merchandising for Musicians" (2012), drawing on his background in engineering and media technology.
